meta.label: p-recalculation: decision error vs consistent t-test (text mode)
meta.ground_truth: synthetic: error planted by construction. Two-tailed p for t(38) = 1.02 is ~0.31 (whole rounding interval sits well above alpha = .05), so reporting p = .003 is a decision error -- FAIL. Two-tailed p for t(30) = 2.50 is ~0.018, so p = .018 is consistent -- PASS.
meta.notes: Neutral, non-biological prose (a generic effect-of-X-on-Y study). One planted statistic-vs-p decision error alongside a correctly reported test guarding against over-flagging.

text: Results. Reaction times in the treatment condition were faster than in the control condition; this contrast reached significance, t(38) = 1.02, p = .003. A separate manipulation check confirmed the expected difference in perceived difficulty, t(30) = 2.50, p = .018. No other comparisons were examined.
